description: Add E2E test coverage for SDK utility functions that have existing e2e-test plugin tools but no E2E tests exercising them: setSessionStorage/getSessionStorage and putJSON/patchJSON/deleteJSON.

userStories.title: Add /api/echo-method endpoint to the E2E test server
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the test server to have an /api/echo-method endpoint that echoes back the HTTP method used so that the sdk_http_methods tool can be tested E2E.
acceptanceCriteria: All responses have Content-Type: application/json and status 200
userStories.notes: Add the endpoint handler to e2e/test-server.ts. Follow the existing pattern used by other /api/* endpoints (e.g., /api/sdk-fetch-test at approximately line 90-100 in test-server-utils.ts, or inline in test-server.ts). The handler should respond to PUT, PATCH, and DELETE methods with { ok: true, method: req.method }. The e2e-test plugin's sdk_http_methods tool (plugins/e2e-test/src/tools/sdk-http-methods.ts) calls putJSON('/api/echo-method', ...), patchJSON('/api/echo-method', ...), and deleteJSON('/api/echo-method', undefined) — so this endpoint must accept all three methods. Check e2e/test-server-utils.ts for how API endpoints are registered and how they handle different methods.

userStories.title: Add E2E tests for setSessionStorage/getSessionStorage SDK utilities
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want E2E tests that exercise the setSessionStorage and getSessionStorage SDK utilities through the full dispatch stack so that regressions in session storage access are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: A new test 'setSessionStorage: writes a value and reads it back' exists in e2e/sdk-utilities.e2e.ts
The test calls the e2e-test_sdk_set_session_storage tool with a key and value
The test asserts that output.ok is true and output.readBack matches the written value
The test verifies the value is in sessionStorage via page.evaluate
userStories.notes:
Add tests to e2e/sdk-utilities.e2e.ts following the existing pattern. The e2e-test plugin already has a sdk_set_session_storage tool (plugins/e2e-test/src/tools/sdk-set-session-storage.ts) that calls setSessionStorage(key, value) and then reads it back with getSessionStorage(key). The tool is already registered and available.

Follow the existing test patterns in e2e/sdk-utilities.e2e.ts:
1. Call setupSdkTest() to get a page with the adapter injected
2. Call callToolExpectSuccess(mcpClient, mcpServer, 'e2e-test_sdk_set_session_storage', { key: 'test-key', value: 'test-value' })
3. Assert output.ok === true and output.readBack === 'test-value'
4. Verify from page context: await page.evaluate(() => sessionStorage.getItem('test-key')) === 'test-value'
5. Close page

userStories.title: Add E2E tests for putJSON, patchJSON, and deleteJSON SDK utilities
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want E2E tests that exercise the putJSON, patchJSON, and deleteJSON SDK utilities through the full dispatch stack so that regressions in HTTP method helpers are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: Three new tests exist in e2e/sdk-utilities.e2e.ts: one each for put, patch, and delete
Each test calls e2e-test_sdk_http_methods with the appropriate method parameter
Each test asserts output.ok is true and output.method matches the expected method
userStories.notes:
Add tests to e2e/sdk-utilities.e2e.ts following the existing pattern. The e2e-test plugin already has a sdk_http_methods tool (plugins/e2e-test/src/tools/sdk-http-methods.ts) that calls putJSON, patchJSON, or deleteJSON against /api/echo-method.

US-001 adds the /api/echo-method endpoint to the test server — this story depends on that endpoint existing.

Follow the existing test patterns:
1. Call setupSdkTest() to get a page
2. For PUT: callToolExpectSuccess(mcpClient, mcpServer, 'e2e-test_sdk_http_methods', { method: 'put', data: { foo: 'bar' } })
3. Assert output.ok === true, output.method === 'put'
4. Repeat for 'patch' and 'delete'
5. Close page after each test

These can be three separate test() calls or a single test with three callTool invocations. Three separate tests is cleaner and matches the existing file's pattern of one assertion per test.
